name: Renal Dysplasia
description: Renal dysplasia is a hereditary condition where the kidneys develop abnormally, with immature or disorganised tissue. Affected puppies develop progressive renal failure at a young age. The severity varies from subclinical to fatal early-onset kidney failure.
symptoms: Signs of uraemia in severe cases
treatment_overview: There is no cure. Medical management follows CKD protocols — dietary modification, phosphate binders, and supportive care. Many affected dogs are euthanised at a young age due to severe renal failure.
prevention_tips: DNA test available for Shih Tzu and Lhasa Apso forms
Renal biopsy of breeding stock in predisposed breeds before mating
